message:
perf(count): aggregate label buckets in SQL for grouped count --by label

Builds on the lean stats-rows path from the prior commit by pushing the
label grouping all the way down to SQLite. Previously, `br count --by label`
still hydrated either the full Issue rows or the lean stats rows, then
walked them in Rust to produce per-label counts via `get_labels_for_issues`.
For workspaces with many labels and many issues the round-trip cost grew
linearly with both axes.

This commit replaces that in-memory bucketing with a single GROUP BY
query.

Storage: `SqliteStorage::count_labels_with_filters`

  SELECT labels.label, COUNT(*)
  FROM issues
  LEFT JOIN labels ON labels.issue_id = issues.id
  WHERE <same predicates as list_issues_filtered>
  GROUP BY labels.label
  ORDER BY labels.label

The WHERE clause mirrors the in-Rust filter logic in
`stats_row_matches_count_filters` exactly, including the trickier
behaviors:

- The `include_closed` / `include_deferred` rule produces
  `status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')` (deferred allowed) or the
  full `('closed', 'tombstone', 'deferred')` exclusion, matching the
  branch in `stats_row_matches_count_filters`.
- When `include_closed` is true and no explicit `statuses` list was
  given, tombstone rows are still hidden — same default as the lean
  filter.
- `include_templates` toggles the `is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL`
  guard.
- `unassigned` accepts both NULL and empty string for assignee.
- `title_contains` uses `LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'` with `escape_like_pattern`,
  same as `list_issues_filtered`.

Label-membership filters branch on whether the rest of the filters can
be evaluated alongside the labels join. When all other filters are
empty (no status/type/priority/assignee/title/updated bounds), we use
the cheaper `append_label_membership_filters` form. Otherwise we
delegate to `label_filter_candidate_ids` first and feed the result
through `append_issue_id_membership_filter`, short-circuiting to an
empty result when the candidate set is empty (so we don't issue an
expensive `IN ()` query that returns the same answer).

The LEFT JOIN means unlabeled issues land in a NULL `labels.label`
bucket, which the row decoder maps to `(no labels)` — matching the
existing in-memory grouping semantics.

CLI wiring: `group_counts_for_filters` short-circuits the
`CountBy::Label` case before the existing dispatcher logic. It pulls
the total via `count_issues_with_filters` (already SQL-side) and the
groups via `count_labels_with_filters`. Status/type/priority/assignee
groupings continue to flow through the lean stats-rows path.

Tests: extends `test_group_counts_label_includes_unlabeled` to also
exercise the new `group_counts_for_filters` path with `CountBy::Label`,
asserting both paths agree on totals (2) and per-bucket counts
(`backend=1`, `(no labels)=1`). The legacy
`group_counts(&storage, &issues, CountBy::Label)` path stays under
test for the cases the SQL aggregate skips.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
